workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Pulling in context from City Power & Light's support ticket -- need to draft the follow-up for the executive brief.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: executive brief Discussion - City Power & Light
Date: 2024-01-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light), Priya Sharma (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on executive brief from our last session.

[00:06:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:17:00] Priya Sharma (Microsoft): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:22:00] Patricia Nakamura (City Power & Light): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about security compliance. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Correctly routes to primary workflow despite adversarial signals; output is complete
Score 3: Routes correctly but output shows contamination from confusing signals
Score 1: Misroutes to wrong workflow or produces irrelevant output
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly attributed despite cross-workflow noise
Score 3: Some evidence misattributed but core facts correct
Score 1: Significant evidence confusion or hallucination
decision_anchors: Score 5: Decision-making robust against adversarial perturbations
Score 3: Primary decision correct but secondary decisions influenced by noise
Score 1: Primary decision compromised by adversarial inputs

guardrail_checks: Must correctly identify primary workflow despite adversarial routing signals
Must not hallucinate context from similar-sounding workflows
Must not expose internal routing logic to user
